title: Contravariant Gobject.obj Implementation Plan
description: Change Gobject.obj from covariant (+'a obj) to contravariant (-'a obj) to enable safe upcasting from derived to base types, eliminating the need for Obj.magic in generated code.

baseline_status.tests: RPC server not running error (infrastructure issue, not code issue)

stages.short_description: Foundation - Change Gobject.obj variance and create variance test suite
stages.description: This stage implements the core type system change and validates it with tests. It involves: (1) Creating a comprehensive variance test suite that demonstrates upcasting works with contravariant obj, (2) Changing type +'a obj to -'a obj in both gobject.ml and gobject.mli, (3) Verifying that upcasting works with the :> operator and that downcasting still correctly fails.

files_to_modify.reason: Change from covariant to contravariant to enable safe upcasting from derived types to base types
Interface must match implementation for variance annotation

files_to_create.description: Comprehensive variance test suite validating upcasting behavior
files_to_create.content_template:
(* Test contravariant Gobject.obj upcasting *)

(* With contravariant -'a obj, we expect: *)
(* - Derived types can be upcast to base types via :> *)

let test_upcast_button_to_widget () =
  (* Button.t has polymorphic variant [`button | `widget | `initially_unowned] *)
  (* Widget.t has polymorphic variant [`widget | `initially_unowned] *)
  (* Button variant ⊆ Widget variant, so Button.t should upcast to Widget.t *)
  let btn = Button.new_ () in
  (* This should compile without Obj.magic *)
  let _ = (btn : Button.t :> Widget.t) in
  ()

let test_upcast_widget_to_initially_unowned () =
  let w = Widget.new_ () in
  let _ = (w : Widget.t :> Initially_unowned.t) in
  ()

let test_nested_upcast () =
  let btn = Button.new_ () in
  (* Direct upcast to base's base *)
  let _ = (btn : Button.t :> Initially_unowned.t) in
  ()

risks.risk: Variance change may break existing type inference in unexpected places
risks.mitigation: Comprehensive test suite validates the change before proceeding
risks.impact: High if wrong, Low if tests pass

risks.risk: Polymorphic variant subtyping may not work as expected with contravariance
risks.mitigation: Phase 0 tests verify the theory before any infrastructure changes
risks.impact: Medium - would require reverting to covariant approach

stages.decision_gate: If variance tests fail with contravariant -'a obj, STOP and investigate. Do NOT proceed to Stage 2.
stages.notes: Note: Plan referenced Initially_unowned.t which doesn't exist in generated namespaces (GObject-only type). Executor adapted with valid alternative tests (Box → Widget, etc.). All tests pass. Variance change validated.

stages.short_description: Generator Modification - Update class_gen_body.ml and regenerate bindings
stages.description: This stage modifies the code generator to emit explicit upcast syntax (obj :> Parent.t) instead of Obj.magic, then regenerates all bindings. It involves: (1) Updating class_gen_body.ml line 57 to use :> instead of Obj.magic, (2) Updating test files that currently use Obj.magic to use the new upcast syntax, (3) Regenerating all 9 namespace bindings, (4) Verifying compilation and test passes.

files_to_modify.current_code: bprintf buf "  inherit %s (Obj.magic obj : %s.t)\n" parent_class_name parent_l1
files_to_modify.reason: Replace unsafe Obj.magic with explicit upcast syntax

files_to_modify.current_code: let as_widget (box : t) : Widget.t = Obj.magic box
files_to_modify.reason: Update test to use safe upcast instead of Obj.magic

files_to_modify.current_code: let box_as_widget (box : Wrappers.Box.t) : Widget.t = Obj.magic box

stages.description: This stage is DEFERRED until Stage 1 and Stage 2 are validated. It updates signal_gen.ml to remove Obj.magic from signal connections. Currently, signal files use two patterns: (1) '(Obj.magic (obj :> _ Gobject.obj) : [`widget] Gobject.obj)' for widget hierarchy, and (2) '(Obj.magic obj :> _ Gobject.obj)' for generic objects. This stage would replace these with proper upcast syntax for signal connections.

files_to_modify.current_code:
let connect_target_expr ~has_widget_parent =
  if has_widget_parent then
    (* Use Obj.magic to coerce widget subtypes to base widget type for signal connection *)
    "(Obj.magic (obj :> _ Gobject.obj) : [`widget] Gobject.obj)"
  else
    "(Obj.magic obj :> _ Gobject.obj)"
files_to_modify.new_code:
let connect_target_expr ~has_widget_parent =
  if has_widget_parent then
    (* Safe upcast to widget type for signal connection *)
    "(obj :> [`widget] Gobject.obj)"
  else
    "(obj :> _ Gobject.obj)"
files_to_modify.reason: Replace Obj.magic with explicit upcast syntax for signal connections
files_to_modify.note: This is a DEFERRED stage - implement only after Stage 1 and Stage 2 are validated

coding_guidelines_applicable.relevance: Variance change improves type safety by making upcasting explicit and compile-time checked
Removing Obj.magic eliminates a partial/unsafe function from generated code
Gobject.mli defines the public interface that must have explicit variance
New variance tests should use Alcotest patterns consistent with existing tests

rollback_plan.trigger: If contravariance causes unexpected issues in production
steps: 1. Revert gobject.ml and gobject.mli to type +'a obj
3. Regenerate all bindings with original generator
4. Revert test file changes back to Obj.magic
5. Document the issues encountered for future reference

references: lablgtk3 Gobject.ml: Reference implementation (uses contravariant -'a obj)
